themes.exampleQuote: Shout out to Bradley who made it so easy for me to schedule our upcoming event.
This brewery has a lot of IPAs: that is a positive for me.
They have a lot of space to spread out; there are other areas you can go to for large groups.
It's been QR codes, then table service, and now suddenly you have to order at the bar, with no clear communication.
I decided to get their Yinser Cheesesteak and I would get it again.

notable_quote: The key: I WILL BE HERE AGAIN.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers keep coming back to two things: a beer selection deep enough to taste through and a floor manager named Bradley who gets mentioned by name in four separate reviews; the friction is inconsistent service logistics, specifically an ordering system that has changed format enough times that at least one regular walked out without ordering.
quarter_narrative: Twenty-one Pittsburgh creators posted 21 TikToks tagging Hop Farm this quarter, generating over 170,000 plays without a single one coming from the brewery's own account, because Hop Farm does not have a TikTok presence. The business is active on Instagram, posting 12 times in the last 30 days with four reels, but that activity is not translating into engagement: a 0.47 percent engagement rate is low for a brewery with this much third-party momentum. In the Pittsburgh Bars family, Hop Farm sits at number two behind Hidden Harbor, whose standout signal is social momentum, exactly the axis where Hop Farm's Instagram output and TikTok absence leave points on the table. Closing the gap to the family leader means converting the creator energy already circling the building into something the account itself can own.
tldr_read: Strong TikTok creator coverage and active Instagram posting, but a 0.47 percent engagement rate and no brewery-owned TikTok account keep it at number two in Pittsburgh Bars. Ones to Watch, number one in Pittsburgh Breweries.
tldr_meaning: The path upward runs through engagement depth, not posting volume; 21 creators and 170,000 TikTok plays exist without the brewery holding any of that reach, and the Instagram activity is not yet producing the interaction rate that would push it past Hidden Harbor in the family standings.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Second In Pittsburgh Bars, With 170K TikTok Plays The Brewery Itself Didn't Post.

playbook.headline: Turn creator footage into owned reach
playbook.action: Launch a Hop Farm TikTok account and repost top creator clips; 21 creators and 170,828 plays exist with no account to capture any of it.

playbook.headline: Post more reels, fewer static frames
playbook.action: Hop Farm posted 12 times in 30 days but only 4 reels; a 0.47 percent engagement rate on a 12-post month signals the format mix is not working.

playbook.headline: Fix and publish the ordering system clearly
playbook.action: At least two reviews cite the rotating QR-to-bar-service changes as friction; posting the current system at the door or on Google removes the single most specific negative pattern in the review set.
